data.title: Kioxia starts shipping samples of 512Gb BiCS FLASHTM 3D TLC devices, aiming for mass production in FY 2025.
data.description: In Tokyo, Kioxia Corporation, a prominent memory solutions provider, revealed that it has started sending out samples of 512Gb Triple-Level Cell (TLC) memory units that utilize its advanced 9th generation BiCS FLASHTM 3D flash memory technology. The company aims to begin large-scale production in the fiscal year 2025. These devices are specifically developed to cater to applications that demand superior performance and remarkable power efficiency within the low to mid-level storage capacities. The 512Gb TLC memory devices are set to be integrated into various systems.

data.title: Solana (SOL) dips to $181 from $200, down 4.56% daily. Key support at $146, bullish momentum remains strong.
data.description: The cryptocurrency SOL is currently trading at $181.81, a decrease of 4.56% in the daily trading. Earlier in the week, it rose above $200. Although experiencing a slight decline in the short term, technical indicators suggest a positive momentum in the market.
